channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Arc Raiders Riven Tides Update Brings New Coastal Map and More
item.content:encoded:
Embark Studios has released the sixth major Arc Raiders update since the game's launch back in October, and it's got a new coastal map for you to explore, plus a new enemy to fight and more.

Following on from March's Flashpoint update, Riven Tides, which is out now across all platforms, boasts the titular map as its main attraction. The Riven Tides map is an abandoned stretch of shoreline with plenty of treasures for you to discover.

You'll also be able to take on the new Arc Turbine enemy, which drifts placidly along until you decide to disturb it, at which point it presents the opportunity for a "totally unique fight". It's a highly defensive enemy that will demand "skill, patience, and nerves" from interested raiders.

Riven Tides is also introducing a new Minor Map Condition by the name of Beachcombing, which will let you sweep a detector across the beach to search for buried treasure. Of course, you may have to expose yourself to danger in order to do so.

All of this is coming alongside new items, a new limited-time event, and a new Raider project. You can check out the full patch notes for the Riven Tides update, which also show the quality-of-life changes that have been made to the game, right here.

If you're not familiar with Arc Raiders, it's a multiplayer PvPvE extraction shooter created by The Finals developer Embark Studios. Like other extraction shooters, you'll descend into a map, battle enemies and other players, and make off with as much loot as you can.

The game has proven wildly popular since launch, racking up over 14 million sales at last count and attracting over 80,000 concurrent players at its 24-hour Steam peak (at time of writing).

If you want to see what all the fuss is about, you can check out Arc Raiders right now on PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
